reasoning: The reference 30-day average for ACLED fatalities in Bangladesh is 31.167. Recent reports from late 2025 (October) indicate that the country is experiencing a 'decade high' in daily homicides (averaging ~11 per day or ~330 per month) and a 40% increase in mob violence. Additionally, extrajudicial killings by security forces have averaged approximately 46 per month since mid-2024. While ACLED only records a subset of homicides (those with a political, communal, or organized violence component), the current elevated violence across the Chittagong Hill Tracts and sustained post-revolutionary instability make it highly probable that fatalities in the 30-day windows will exceed the baseline. The first two dates (Nov 2 and Nov 25) are close to the current spike in violence, while the January 2026 date is further out and thus carries more uncertainty, though the baseline political situation remains volatile.
